authoritative_sources.title: Applying for a Film Permit
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FilmLA is the official film office for Greater Los Angeles and this knowledge-base article defines application timing, permit-assignment workflow, permit-release dependencies, and insurance verification for location filming.

authoritative_sources.title: Area Requirements: City of Los Angeles
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official area-specific production guidance naming permit requirements, filming hours, insurance thresholds, no-parking posting deadlines, and department requirements for Los Angeles shoots.

authoritative_sources.title: Producer Portal
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SAG-AFTRA workflow page describing signatory communications, document statuses, cast and production documents, security-deposit handling, and post-production paperwork for union-covered productions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Performer signatory and document workflow review

authoritative_sources.title: Production Center | Television Live Action Forms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SAG-AFTRA production-center page linking producer guides, signatory instructions, and post-production reporting forms for live-action television workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Production paperwork and post-production reporting setup
used_for: music cue and post-report routing boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Deal Memos & Reports Compliance Forms
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official DGA employer reference organizing project-specific signatory packages, deal memos, and reports-compliance forms by production type including theatrical, television, SVOD, commercials, and new media.

authoritative_sources.title: Reports Compliance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official DGA department page stating that contractually required documents such as deal memos, post-production addenda, quarterly gross earnings reports, employment data reports, and weekly work lists are necessary to protect member rights.
used_for: signatory company and episode-level data expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Safety Bulletins
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized industry safety reference from the motion picture and television labor-management safety committee, listing current bulletins for firearms, helicopters, stunts, and other production hazards and stating they may be attached to call sheets.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Safety evidence and special-activity control review

authoritative_sources.title: Performing Arts and Motion Picture Hazard Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OSHA hazard-information sheet naming core safety-program elements, leading injury events, leading citations, and control resources for performing arts and motion picture industries.

authoritative_sources.title: TPN Quick Start Guide for Service Providers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official TPN guidance stating that TPN is an MPA-owned industry content-security initiative, that the assessment program is built on MPA Content Security Best Practices, and that TPN+ is the centralized registry for service-provider security information.

workflow_stages.name: Jurisdiction and production-profile intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify jurisdiction, production type, release model, production phase, special activities, and governing client or studio rules before classifying the request as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope.

workflow_stages.name: Permit and location-control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map filming obligations to permit applications, area requirements, insurance thresholds, posting deadlines, neighborhood notifications, and department approvals for the exact location plan.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Signatory and labor workpaper review
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether SAG-AFTRA and DGA signatory records, deal memos, cast lists, day-out-of-days, time reports, and clearance steps exist with traceable ownership and status.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Safety and special-activity evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether call-sheet safety bulletins, special-activity notices, incident-prevention controls, and hazard-specific approvals support the planned activity or require escalation.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Content-security and vendor-assurance review
workflow_stages.description: Review TPN status, client security addenda, asset-handling controls, and vendor evidence before enabling dailies, screeners, cloud collaboration, or remote-post workflows.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, remediation, and boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Convert missing approvals, source conflicts, schedule pressure, and adjacent-authority requests into escalation packets that preserve evidence and refuse unsupported signoff.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Permit applications, area-requirements extracts, certificates of insurance, neighborhood notifications, and temporary no-parking posting requests
Police, fire, special monitor, drone, aircraft, firearms, stunt, water, vehicle, animal, and special-effects approval records or notices
SAG-AFTRA signatory applications, performer contracts, Exhibit G time reports, cast lists, day-out-of-days, final cast-clearance records, and security-deposit tracking
DGA signatory package materials, deal memos, weekly work lists, employment data reports, post-production addenda, and project classification records
Call sheets, daily production reports, safety meeting records, CSATF safety bulletins, incident logs, and remediation trackers
Studio or streamer security addenda, TPN assessment artifacts, shield status, vendor questionnaires, NDA and watermark controls, and asset-handling logs
Tenant authority maps, approval matrices, and policy overlays for permits, labor documentation, special activities, security, and executive exceptions
systems_of_record: Film-office or permit portal such as MyFilmLA or local equivalent
Guild or union producer portal and document repository
Payroll, startwork, and production accounting system
Document control or GRC evidence repository
Safety incident log or risk register
Vendor-risk, TPN+, or client security review system

allowed: Map production-specific obligations to permits, workpapers, safety controls, content-security evidence, owners, and review cadence.
Prepare evidence-backed readiness, gap, audit-prep, and remediation summaries for production compliance workflows.
Escalate conflicts, missing prerequisites, and adjacent-authority requests without overstating permit, legal, labor, or safety authority.
escalate: Missing jurisdiction, production type, union coverage, special-activity profile, or client-security requirement set.
Conflicting permit conditions, union paperwork rules, client security controls, or tenant policy.
Requests that imply legal interpretation, permit response, guild signoff, broadcaster-compliance certification, rights clearance, or licensed safety authority.
refuse: Any legal advice, permit interpretation, or formal guild or union opinion.
Formal signoff on permit release, labor clearance, payroll, rights clearance, safety plan approval, or content-security certification.
Decisions to proceed with special activities, location use, or vendor access when required evidence is missing.

domain_failure_modes: Production proceeds without required permit, insurance, posting, or department-approval evidence.
Cast or crew start work while signatory, deal-memo, or cast-clearance documentation remains incomplete.
Schedule pressure causes safety bulletins, community notices, or special-activity controls to be skipped or normalized away.
Pre-release content moves through dailies, screener, or cloud-post workflows without the required client or TPN security evidence.
The lane drifts into broadcaster standards-and-practices, rights clearance, payroll approval, or legal interpretation because the industry label is broad.
The pack stays generic and fails to request real production records such as permits, call sheets, deal memos, and vendor-security artifacts.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence handling when required facts or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Municipal, state, and country-specific filming rules for child performers, firearms, drones, traffic control, and special effects vary materially by jurisdiction
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane therefore requires exact shoot jurisdiction and special-activity context before answering and routes interpretation to permit, safety, or legal owners when local rules materially change the outcome.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Guild and union obligations vary by agreement type, budget tier, format, and release model across theatrical, television, SVOD, and new-media productions
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The package stays validated by requiring the exact applicable agreement and signatory status before giving a compliance output, rather than guessing from generic production labels.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Broadcast standards-and-practices and FCC-facing obligations do not always sit inside production compliance even when the industry includes broadcast
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane treats on-air standards, caption certification, sponsorship-identification, and similar broadcaster-distribution duties as adjacent until the tenant explicitly assigns them.

notes: The public baseline intentionally focuses on production permitting, labor workpapers, set safety, and content-security evidence because those are the records that make this lane materially different from generic compliance support.
Tenant retrieval must add project-specific union terms, municipal rules, studio or streamer requirements, and client security addenda before the lane can make bounded readiness statements.
